Is it possible that whenever we get GAME CHANGING bugs in the game, like the Firebomb Knockback on Sloops ONLY , that a patch/hotfix be put into place immediately instead of waiting for an entire update?! That is one thing that actually baffles me as to why they are left in the game for such a long period of time. And once the "exploit" becomes common knowledge, other crews use it against you constantly. Sloops are already at an extreme disadvantage towards other ships and it's just disheartening that it's been left in the game for so long. I know it's getting patched out this next update, but again, it should have been patched out the moment it was reported when a fix was ready for it.
It starts at 11:00 PM

Answering this one straight off the bat to set some expectations - sometimes these things are not a quick fix. Sometimes they take investigation because they have come into place because of a change and are unintended so we need to find what changes made these things not work as intended, which is a lot of work from the team. It isn't that they are not prioritised. It's that they are a BIG task to figure out what has caused it and then implement a fix that also doesn't break something else. These things are not left in longer on purpose but bugs are fixed when we have fix for them,. Finding a fix for them requires investigations. (edited)

Sorry if this has been asked already and I missed it, is there any way that more severe penalties can be put into place for game pass users who just create new accounts to continue harassment etc? I'm guessing that's nothing you have control of here and it's an xbox thing, but it seems like there's no consequence to getting banned at all and as much as I love game pass and think it's an incredible service, it does mean that reporting bad elements of the community is next to pointless as they are up and running again within minutes.

Just off the top of my head, a hardware ban resulting in the user having to buy game pass again but at a non discounted rate. I'm assuming that the people I'm referring to bring more harm than good to the xbox and pc community so removing them would surely increase revenue for xbox in the long run

So, Xbox bans are a thing, but, to my understanding, we can't ban a PC. I don't know the details but it's not within our control as it's not like an Xbox which falls under Microsoft so if you break ToS it's gone. I have no doubt that Rob and the team use everything in their ability to remove players but it is an ongoing battle to stay up to date with how they do it and to take action, which is where the reports are invaluable - one report could lead to us spotting 4,5,6 alts off that same account and also actioning those. It is something we are taking very seriously though and are working on literally every day right now to try and keep you guys and the devs safe. I know this isn't the perfect answer, but I hope it sheds a bit of light on what we're trying to do.
metallic silver ship set? if we're even humoring requests lol (I know that's not how this works, I'm just at the point of begging ) (edited)

So I submitted a ticket for someone cheating using hacks and this was the paste-reply: Thanks for your patience while we investigated your request. This has now been passed to our Security and Test teams for further investigation. Please note that, as this will be investigated internally, we are unable to disclose the eventual outcome of the report. For a while we were getting confirmation when action was taken against a player we reported, has this policy changed? (edited)

I would like to know if action was taken so I know if what I saw is enough evidence for something to happen. I know the response will be "always just report players if you suspect cheating". This isn't free however, it takes a good 15-20 minutes of my free time to scrub the VOD to find the correct timestamp to put in the report, watch portions of the VOD so I can accurately describe what happened and to write up the ticket itself when things might not be as obvious to someone with less time in the game than me. If action isn't being taken than I rather not be wasting my time. (edited)
This is regarding ticket #403942 if someone wants to specifically look at what was reported. (edited)
Are there any plans to prevent people cancelling Legend of the Veil to prevent it being stolen?
I don't believe so. If you chased someone for another voyage and they cancelled it the same thing would happen - it's their voyage.
It's not like a world event.

On other voyages, you can't complete them unless they've started them something, such as a Skeleton crew spawn. It's frustrating to see a giant tornado in the sky that can be see across the map, and then they just cancel it when they lose. Sort of defeats the object of having it visible across the entire map?
I dunno why this is so designed to see it over the whole map, when you can just cancel it when someone is attacking you. Even when you sink the crew, they leave the server and the voyage is gone.

Yeah agreed, as an example, if you cancel a Gold Vault if someone is attacking you when the vault is open. All the loot and the vault stays open. If you attack someone doing an OOS mission and they cancel the voyage whilst killing skeletons, the skeletons remain so you can still steal them. If you attack someone as they're doing the new Merchant missions (infact all merchant missions) if they cancel the shipwreck and loot remains. So why does the Athena Tornado instantly get removed if someone cancels the voyage, when this is supposed to be "end tier" content? I think the tornado should persist for atleast 30-40 minutes, and all loot persist even if the crew who spawned it cancels the voyage.

@j0toro may I have an answer to my question please? Has there been a policy shift from what it was previously to where we are now no longer informed as to if action has been taken against players that we report or does that only apply to reports of toxicity and not reports of cheating/hacking?
Jo has a day off today just fyi.

I believe the only rule is that what ever you do on a private server must be possible on a public server. For example if you use 6 galleons on a custom server for a SoT shot, that wouldn’t be allowed as that’s currently impossible on public servers. (edited)

I support this 100%. The Veil of Legends Voyage is just that... a voyage. If someone is doing a Gold Hoarder Vault and they vote down their voyage before they get their vault key, you can't be upset about that and feel entitled to their key somehow. If you want to steal from this voyage, be stealthy about it. It's far too easy to steal as it is already.
Hard disagree. If you get the key for a Gold vault, then you can open the vault as the voyage is done. You have no means of digging it up yourself without having the map. Which you can't get.

Problem with this voyage is that there is no key. Completing the voyage is the key itself, which requires you (the thief) to be stealthy enough to let them complete the voyage. The way I see it is this... I'm tucked on an Ashen Athena Voyage and they're about to go dig up their Ashen Athena when they notice me on their ship. They get spooked and either log out of the game completely or they vote down the voyage. There is no way for me to go get that chest now. It's gone. And that's how I see this voyage. This new voyage is so easy to steal and when the final garrison on the fort is destroyed, the Athena chest and loot literally just "appear" out of nowhere. It's not locked behind a door. It doesn't require you to put the veil stone in the mask. It just appears. Super EZ Mode. And right now, these voyages are STILL popping up all over the place, so if someone votes down one, the chances of you landing on your next server with another Athena Emissary is extremely high.

Voting for the Adventure itself only took 1 person. Voting down the Adventure on the table took a majority vote, so that 3 man scenario in that screenshot would not have worked. However, here is how the exploit DID work... Brig: 1 person votes for the Adventure and stays at Merrick while the other 2 players stay at Cannon Cove with the boat. They talk to the NPC and dig up the supplies and then vote down the voyage. Person at Merrick starts it back up. Rinse and repeat. Problem with this is that the supplies from Cannon Cove aren't worth the same amount as the ones from Smuggler's Bay, but if you stack them up enough, it really doesn't matter... you can definitely influence the vote. Now from the Reaper's PoV, you can blow any random rowboat you find out on the seas, but like the 1st supplies from Cannon Cove, they aren't worth that much point wise. Now UNLIKE Merrick's Adventure, the Reaper's couldn't be cheesed/exploited so easily because it required the rowboat that the Servant of the Flame gave you and you had to be within a certain proximity of the skulking figures bush with that rowboat in order to interact with them. So for example, you couldn't leave someone on the beach of Cannon Cove or Twin Groves with a random rowboat waiting for the Skulking Figure. They just won't appear/interact without the rowboat from the Servant of the Flame. Now I trust @j0toro more than anyone and if she says it was not rigged, I believe her. But do I think it may have been compromised from the cheesing/exploiting? Perhaps. We will NEVER know without knowing the total of Cannon Cove vs Smuggler's Bay supplies that were turned in. (edited)

@j0toro TL:DR would you please be willing to inquire as to if all loot items in the game could be placed on the longer 15 minute timer before they start to sink in open water? We have two weird lists of items, one on a 6 minute timer and the other on a 15 minute timer. 6 minutes just isn't enough time to recover the loot in most PvP scenarios that involve 3 ships. 15 minutes however usually is. I can't count how many times I've sunk a ship, then gotten 3rd partied by another ship, sunk the new ship and then go back to the 1st ships loot to find the majority of the loot has already sunk and despawned leaving only the merchant and ghost loot still floating. I understand server performance is always a big consideration but I can't imagine this having a massive impact.

I would think the original reasoning was that a crew could not complete their merchant animal voyage if their crates sunk so all merchant loot was just flagged to be on the longer 15 minute timer. The list includes an odd set of other items too, ghost loot, fort keys, kegs, grog chests. It's just not intuitive at all to the player which is which. The other weird part is also that the land despawn timers are also different. Items that sink in 6 minutes take 3 hours to despawn on land but items that sink in 15 minutes actually have a shorter land despawn timer of 2 hours. It's just weird and unintuitive. It would just be really nice if every single item in the game could be put on the longer timers of 15 minutes to sink and 3 hours to despawn on land so it's all consistent.
Happy to raise this one, yes
Gotta ask now before your channel grows any more and you leave us all to become a full-time streamer.
she'd need to find better mods for the channel first imho...

do you also speak about red sea runner? It's very annoying and it's more often the last months, that people suicide in the red seas. Couldn't you make the loot float back/port to the normal map or any other ideas about that problem? @Sonicbob (Drew) (edited)
This is in no way planned, consider it on the list

Will ensure the team are aware. We know that runners are a frustration point but clearly there is a balance to be struck here to allow legitimate scenarios while working to address the topic of red sea loot and endless chases etc.

adding my feelings on this, "running" is a legit playstyle as no one is entitled to a fight or anything to do with a crew if they don't want to. Red Sea loot though is definitely a problem, and someone doing this should forfeit their loot some other way (loot being pushed back in, or even make use of the Ocean Treasury mermaid mechanic etc) to place the loot in a safe location. My personal thoughts on how to combat running as a more fair and engaging game mechanic for both parties is to just have the wind change more often and more drastically (for example a wind change would be more than 30 degrees on every time it changes). I feel like keeping on top of changing sails is a skill gap mechanic and crews should be on top of both positioning and wind/sail management.

I agree it’s a valid tactic the same way camping is. Some may not agree with it but it’s still a thing. I agree there should be some sort of penalty for loot you have if you do this. It shouldn’t necessarily be an automatic other crew gets everything easily but something that gives a risk/reward. Maybe something like if you red sea it disconnects you from the game and takes your loot and puts it on a shipwreck that shows for everyone else on the server (reaper wreck or something). Now I don’t think work work as-is since people would just steal peoples ships and Red Sea them to get them to dc or something but you get the idea.

I think the best way to invalidate the "red sea-ing" tactic would be to add a true/false flag (bool) to all loot items of "abandoned". If a ship sinks within the devil's shroud damage area or goes all the way off of the edge of the map then all loot aboard their ship is flagged as abandoned. In addition to this to counter players throwing their loot overboard prior to sinking, any piece of loot that is in the devil's shroud damage area is also flagged as abandoned. Now when the ship despawns after sinking all loot that that crew owned (owning being either in their ship or they were the last crew to pick up the item) then all items flagged with being abandoned are immediately then teleported to the nearest "normal" water map square and also marked with a thick bird flock (the kind that goes over shipwrecks). This doesn't prevent any ship from running away or sinking their ship within the devil's shroud but it basically completely prevents them from denying their attackers any of their loot. (edited)

On a larger point of addressing running in general I think just an complete overhaul of the sailing mechanics would be needed that would be a significant amount of work. As Pace points out I think there should be a larger skill gap to sailing to make both chasing and running a more involved process that is more exciting for both sides. However I don't believe increasing the skill gap necessarily has to make sailing unintuitive, rather I think sailing could actually be made more intuitive to the player even if it is slightly more complex than it currently is. Some of this would actually be making the sailing more like real world physics. A few points: -Get rid of "center sailing" This is absolutely not at all intuitive to new players. The sloop specifically shouldn't have such a large speed boost going against the wind by using this tactic that logically does not make any sense. -Balance ships top speeds Specifically the brigantine vs the sloop but all ships in general really need a balancing pass. Honestly for fairness I do think all ships should actually have the exact same top speed, but only when all sails are angled with the wind correctly. This gives the sloop the maneuverability advantage as it only has 1 sail that turns quicker, larger ships have more crew to move sails making this balanced.

-Greater speed bonuses for tacking Rather than only one speed bonus when the sails have billow I think there should be an exponential gain to speed based on the percentage of wind that is being caught. This could create scenarios where when chasing another ship with crosswind that actually turning the ship out to get more wind would provide enough of a speed boost that they would actually close on the enemy ship faster than they would by just sailing directly toward them. This would increase the skill gap of sailing while still remaining intuitive to the player. -More wind shifts As Pace points out simply having the wind shift more frequently and by a greater amount each time is a very simple way of increasing the skill gap. -"Stealing" wind This was actually a real world sailing tactic of getting behind an enemy ship going straight with the wind. The wind that is caught by the attacking ship actually reduces the amount of wind that reaches the enemy ship which actually reduces their top speed. Similarly if two ships are going against the wind than the running ships sails are reducing the amount of wind reaching the pursuing ship, reducing it's drag from the wind and making the running ship slower. In terms of practical game mechanics it's just like drafting in car racing games. I understand this would be a very large undertaking and not just a simple fix to one or two of the games sailing mechanics. However I really do thing the game would be massively improved by a sailing overhaul so I hope it can be considered at some point. (edited)

I love the season, but I STRONGLY feel that 3 things need to be changed. #1 - Sovereigns shouldn't accept ANY Athena's Fortune loot whatsoever. Players should still have to make the trek to the tavern and sell that loot to the Mysterious Stranger. #2 - Spyglassing ships and revealing the ship name and player name. Sorry for my bluntness, but that is the STUPIDEST thing in this game right now. I literally spyglassed a ship that was out of render distance and it still gave me the name of the ship and the gamertag. I'm sorry Rare, but I understand that the Playerlist Hack was patched over, but you literally just gave cheaters/stream snipers a Rare Made Tool on a silver platter. #3 - Replacing Gamertags changes the name of my ship on my screen. What's the point of even buying my own ship and naming my own ship if I can't enjoy the name? Everyone else out on the seas can see the name of my ship, except for me, the owner. And if people see the same hidden name that I see, it's still a dead giveaway that the person is a streamer, not to mention that again, the spyglass shows the gamertag of the person as an incredible distance. #2's & #3's were BOTH brought up in concern if I'm not mistaken and they were both ignored. The rest of the update... PHENOMENAL!!!! Great job on the rest, but these things, especially #2 and #3 have to change.

I think I already know the answer to this but just want to clarify. Any innuendo or sexually suggestive ship name will result in a player having the right to name a ship revoked from them, is this statement correct? I've had a few people claim that they didn't even name their ship anything bad, but the Internet is full of people getting in trouble for "things they didn't do"

It likely will yes. Ted Talk incoming but hopefully it will help some of you as I know you're on the front line. This isn't an 'official statement' from us but is the culmination of CMing here for 18 months, working with support, the community code of conduct and multiple years working with game age rating categorising in retail and head offices... SoT is a 12+/T rating mainly because it is online. In the UK this rating also extends to 'mild violence' and can include adult themes, this does not specify NSFW or sexual in nature but would cover things such as PvP, harder hitting lore sections, character death etc. In the US their interpretation and rating of 'Teen' also states 'crude humour' but nowhere in our game is crude humour anything sexual or hinting at anything sexual, NSFW etc. When people play in an open world environment they are responsible for what they put out there, what others are seeing as part of their experience and they are responsible for upholding the family values we and Microsoft adhere to. Anything that is seen to be breaking this that is reported may result in action being taken if it doesn't follow the values of our game. Thanks for coming to me TedTalk. I hope this helps (edited)
It's not my place to outright comment on in game moderation but I work VERY closely with CS and we don't ban for no reason. They have tools and hefty training and rules to follow.
What does "suspended with a final warning" mean? Yellowbeard with a final chance?
Yup. Means they've either messed up BAD or have previous infractions
Usually the latter

Ha. We have to follow various guidelines so likely action taken in line with that

in that case, can we get a statement (at least in here) about using black screens to get back to your ship? there was a discussion about it in #tavern during the charity stream.
starts here, so you don't have to look for it: https://discordapp.com/channels/550315686636027908/968542238864994416/1025418206581629009
Taking a look and discussing this one now

OK, so black screen mechanic exists to help players who are stuck in some of our terrain - hence why they are usually corners, rocks or underneath trees, structures etc. We know that this is a widely used tactic for players to return to their ship easily and would never say that people can't use it as it's a needed feature to stop players having to alt+f4 or exit the game should they become stuck as a solo player with no blunderbombs or grog (neither of which are guaranteed to work). There will always be spots that this can be triggered given it's purpose as a player support tool. Without overhauling how the 'get unstuck' system works, which is not on anyone's list right now, it would be impossible to police and unfair to say players shouldn't use this tactic to get back to their ship when in a sticky situation. We would ask that going forward this isn't showcased on our official channels, as it isn't really using them as intended and there should always be an awareness of the channel that content is being shown on, but wouldn't ever extend that to anyone's personal channels for content creation. However, we're also not going to drag anyone over the coals should they slip up as we know this is utlised by many and sometimes changing that mindset can be tricky and people are human and just forget when certain habits are built.

It's probably also worth mentioning that the player in question is not a content creator or partner so likely uses this mechanic everyday in their day to day playing. It's highly unlikley they are in the 'content creator' mindset of 'I'm on SoT TV and should not do this.' so I would defintiely give him the benefit of the doubt here.
oh i 100% don't blame them or wanted to get anyone into trouble. was just unclear if people should be using it on streams since AA's were basically told not to advertise it. Thank you

Yeah I would definitely say if you're making content in an official capacity then please don't but I also know this player isn't a content creator so can't really go ham at this. I think it's a 'situational awareness' piece more than anything

It's based on win/lose but also takes into account the whole crew - one newer player (or in the case of a skilled crew where one hasn't played S8 yet is is on their second account and hasn't played yet) the matchmaking will look at them overall. Combined with if they won or lost their previous match and if the newer crew have been on a streak, crews will still meet other crews that are not in their ranking. We want to offer Allegiance for players that get run out of bounds after a long battle and losing. In these cases players earn no Allegiance and they can have spent from 10 - 50 minutes fighting and then just get boarded, ran out of bound and earn nothing for all of the time investment they have spent in; this is causing lots of frustration for players and ultimately will deter them playing for the long term. We acknowledge that this allows for players to farm a small amount of Allegiance for running out of the bounds. We will monitor this closely and act accordingly. You will still be rewarded ALL rewards for defeating emergent opposing faction ships, including Champions (Allegiance, Emissary Rep, Streak, Hourglass Value etc). The only exception is when two ships are in an invasion battle vs each other. Third party ships will not be rewarded for joining in. This is done to dissuade crews teaming up unfairly. We are monitoring the average wait times for Defending crews when waiting for Battle. This should be improved with Same Faction Battles and Cross Stamp Migration. Regardless we will continue to monitor the data for the average wait times here and see what improvements can be made. We only have so many resources we can assign to any one given task. We've done ALOT of updates for Season 8 already and will continue to monitor what is required in the future. (edited)

I will say, we had a queued up Athena ship try to steal our veil Voyage the other night. Got into a fight, sunk them right before they were invaded and their opponent turned to fight us instead. So we sunk them too. Not being able to get Allegiance for those organic fights feels a bit like punishment. Is there any way for the game to know if another ship began fighting you prior to them being invaded? (edited)

@Andrew Preston Good to know on the other bits but there definitely needs to be more logic in place for players sailing out of bounds and receiving rep. Loss farming is already extremely prevalent on NA West servers, this is absolutely going to make it worse as now players can simply queue up and then literally go AFK to gain allegiance. I understand trying award players in legitimate matches that just get driven out of bounds while being spawn camped. The problem here is there's no code logic to determine the difference between someone in this scenario vs someone just queueing up and going AFK. I don't see this as being overly complicated, any one of these actions could mark the ship as "engaged" in battle and eligible to earn allegiance from going out of bounds: -Killed by enemy player in last 60 seconds. -Boarded by enemy player in last 90 seconds. -Wheel damaged by enemy cannons in last 120 seconds. -Knocked off of ship by enemy cannons or thrown blunderbombs in last 120 seconds. -Harpooned off of the ship by an enemy harpoon in the last 120 seconds. These are legitimate scenarios where a player might be driven out of bounds. Without any one of these triggers occurring players should not be awarded allegiance for going out of bounds because they are most likely just AFK loss farming. Loss farming in general needs to be addressed as it absolutely ruins the experience for players looking for a legitimate fight.
I understand, we’ll monitor it - thanks for raising as a concern.

I think a change was made to prevent Alliance servers from farming Rep/Allegiance outside of diving and teaming up on other ships.

And now it was changed to an active fight, meaning that both ships dove and paired off against one another or one ship is defending and is invaded by another. Any other ships in the open world attacking one another outside of those 2 scenarios receive no credit at all, but the losing ship still merges to another server.

Which I'm perfectly fine with because now with Season 8, if I want a fight where I know the person can't run from me, I can just dive. If I'm doing my own thing on a server and not diving and I come across another ship that's opted in invasion but they attack me instead and they sink, I don't have to worry about them coming back over and over because they'll just get merged. Win-Win in my book.

@j0toro @Andrew Preston I would like to know if anything is planed to be done about loss farming, or at the very least that this is acknowledged as being a legitimate problem that should be addressed. It's only getting worse... v2.7.3 / NA-West / 8:00PM - 4:00AM / Solos 103 Matches Played 47 Loss Farmers 45% Loss Farmer Ratio This is ruining the hourglass PvP experience in the solos bracket. The idea behind loss reputation is not discourage casual / low skill players from quitting hourglass PvP which would remove players from the matchmaking pool and increase matchmaking times. If you are looking for legitimate fights then matching vs a loss farmer is just wasting your time and you have to requeue in the hopes of getting an actual fight, if you look at it from this perspective loss farmers are effectively making the queue times longer . When we first talked about Season 8 you said you didn't want to see this feature take the route of Arena, you wanted to support this feature not just for Season 8 but for years to come. Loss farming is destroying this feature for solo players and we're barely a few months into it. I've already had multiple people in my chat say they have quit hourglass PvP entirely because of loss farmers. '

I understand that there is no simple solution to this problem that isn't also perceived as hurting players that are legitimately trying but are simply not good at PvP. Something needs to be done though. I still think no gains after a 5 loss streak is the best route. Diminishing returns on loss allegiance coupled with a bonus to allegiance on the next win might be more encouraging to players to keep trying. I would personally prefer there just be 0 gains for losing period but I know that isn't going to be considered. Much more complicated routes can be taken such as a hidden scoring system that weights participation such as cannon shots landed, buckets bailed, etc. with a minimum score required to earn any allegiance. I've already made multiple suggestions for addressing this issue and someone always argues with them. The core of the problem is that you've created a system to where unless you are a top tier PvP-er than it is literally faster just to loss farm than is to even so much as try to win. You get 25% of a win's allegiance for losing. Assume a 2 minute average queue time, 2 minutes to burn your own ship down when the match starts, 1 minute to sink / respawn / anchor up / requeue, you can farm a loss every 5 minutes. 4 losses in 20 minutes is the equivalent allegiance of 1 win. If you try legitimately to win a single match might take 20 minutes or longer for you to still just lose anyway. Intentionally losing absolutely should not be the META for gaining allegiance. ----- (edited)

Another issue that needs to be addressed in solos is "Invite For Win". The fix for this is very straight forward however and should not be controversial in any way. Player A and B get matched in an hourglass fight. Player A invites player B to their crew. Player B accepts the invite which causes their ship to instantly scuttle because they have left the session. Player A then wins the match because Player B scuttled. The rewards are delayed enough for Player B to load into Player A's crew, both Player A and B get allegiance and commendation credit for the win. This has simple fix: Any player invited to the crew of a solo sloop that is in an active hourglass PvP fight should not be given any rewards for the solo player winning the match.

I have asked BI for data on this and am waiting for them to get back to me. I have asked for ship that enter hourglass and don't fire cannons as well as solo sloopers who join the other crew in a fight. Until we have this data we can't act or make further comment.
I should have it by the end of this week so will pick it up internally then
I'm also going to raise the issue of the 'ferry banned' player getting rep cos that seems like an easy 'fix' for the 'invite for win' scenario
Will speak to Andrew about it ❤️

Simply "not firing cannons" isn't going to give an accurate account of how bad this is, although you should see a whole lot of returns on it. Lots of loss farmers do fire cannons, they attempt to make it appear as though they are trying when they aren't. They will go into a direct broadside with you, even fire cannons, but then simply never bail water or repair their ship and don't return from the ferry if killed. "0 buckets bailed" would probably be somewhat more accurate. A better search might just be raw numbers. Look for players with 500+ losses and less than 10% wins. Legitimate players should not be this bad at PvP after that amount of experience. You could see how many players already have a curse from loss farming "Level 100 in Guardians or Servients with 1000+ losses and <100 wins". (edited)
There are a whole lot though, probably the majority, that just fully raise sails when the match starts and then just emote on the canopy.

It's really bad at this point. Anecdotally from talking with my chat it appears to be much worse later at night and much worse on NA servers than it is on EU servers. It's absolutely ruining the experience of hourglass PvP for me personally, every match I get into now I have to say "Let's hope we get someone that tries". It feels like it's RNG if I can even get a legitimate fight now, like rolling the dice and saying "Come on! No loss farmers!" like I'm shooting craps at a casino.

Yeah defender queues have always seemed to become broken or at the least extremely inconsistent. Sometimes you get invaded every 20-30 minutes, other times you spend 3+ hours on the same server and never get invaded once. Whatever it is something is definitely going on. I'd still say IMO the whole system would be a lot more fun if the matchmaking priority is Defender vs Attacker instead of Attacker vs Attacker. That should at least lower defender queue times but there also definitely appears to be a bug or technical issue that prevents defenders from ever being invaded in certain servers / scenarios.

I'm poking around to see who I can ask so I'm on it and will let you know ASAP I have a feeling that attacker vs attacker will always be prioritised though cos, well, I love you all but if it's not you'll all be in here complaining about tunnel times
Defenders are definitely getting invaded but I've asked for some stats

I don't see how Attacker vs Defender increases the queue times at all. The system tries to match Attacker vs Defender, if none are available then it matches Attacker vs Attacker. I don't see how this has to add any additional time to the queues, it's just based on what's available.

Like right now say 6 crews queue up at the same time: 5 attackers, 1 defender. The system prioritizes A vs A matches: A vs A A vs A A vs D (because no attackers are available). If defender battles were the priority then it's flipped: A vs D A vs A (because no defenders are available) A vs A (because no defenders are available) The overall queue times for all crews are exactly the same . On a large scale however what this means is that defenders will overall have a much shorter queue times while attackers have the exact same queue times they currently do (as defenders cannot be queued vs other defenders). It would massively help defenders and make absolutely no difference to attackers. Is there something I'm missing in this logic?

I'd assume the people I talk to when I say I will get more information will likely have the logic which is why I said I will ask them. It's their system so speculation doesn't help when you don't have the insight

I've got a community member who has been getting spawn trapped (not camped -- they're getting bucketed/rep'd to be kept afloat) in hourglass fights. They didn't want to scuttle and forfeit loss XP. They've reported with video evidence several times and been told in response nothing is wrong with the behavior. They even have people saying on hotmics that they have "pent up aggression" and they're "sorry" for spawn trapping. Is this or is this not a ToS/Conduct breach that is reportable? They've given up on reporting it, and reporting in general, sadly, but it'd be good to know for future reference.

So long as the evidence lines up with ToS or community code of conduct breach, which spawncamping does (see below) we would 100% take action on those accounts. Either the evidence provided isn't enough context (not long enough, doesn't show gamertags etc) or they're not telling you the full truth. We don't tolerate bullying, harrassment or spawncamping. It would rarely result in a redbeard for a first offence, but would result in a yellowbeared of at least 48 hours, more if this wasn't their first offence. (edited)

When reporting it's important to include evidence, this should be a video of at least several minutes long and Gamertags and comms should be included. It should be sent via dropbox/Googledrive link and NOT via YouTube as even if private YT can strike your channel for racist terms (if the language goes that far). All reports are looked at by our agents in house and decisions are made based off what evidence is provided. If our agents are saying 'no action was taken' then that's likely down to the evidence not showing enough context.

I thought so. I'll try to encourage them to give reporting another go. It's two people I trust a lot (a mod and a regular, who is super sweaty) who dm'd me about this. Don't want this to put them off getting their curses.

100% and it shouldn't. I totally get how frustrating it must be for them but please reassure them we don't take actions like that lightly but also we HAVE to have solid evidence to take action against someones account
I did. I'll also check out the clips if they're comfy sending them to me. Appreciate the quick reply, j0 ❤️

So if, for example, they send a 30 second clip of them dying twice but dont show the other crew repairing or bucketing - that could just be the opposing crew 'holding down the ship' until they get holes from whoever is on cannons. I would advise them to set up their Windows+G/Nvidia Shadowplay recording to at least the previous 5 minutes, and to also look at maybe using OBS to record hourglass battles which I KNOW is excessive but if they're being targeted then it's the best way to ensure we have all the tools to take action

If the Obsidian Ship Crest is unlocked, do us Partners have to watch other streams to earn it? Or will it be applied to our accounts like normal drops?

-Are Reaper's chests available (by any method) on saver seas? I really think these should only be on high seas as their whole design was to be contested in pvp. If players can just server hop on private servers over and over to just farm these it removes the incentive of anyone ever trying to contest them on the high seas. -Are all world events (other than FOF and FOTD) available on saver seas? Again I really think that all world events should be limited to the high seas only. World events were always at the core pvp experience in sot and I think players should have to go to the high seas to get these experiences, hopefully with an expectation that they might be contested. If players can just do all of them on saver seas they again likely will have little motivation to do them on the high seas. Sea Forts were specifically designed to give a fort raid experience in a more casual way, I would think these are enough for saver seas and that the other events should be limited to high seas. -Will there be a lifetime hard cap on gold earned on saver seas? Reputation is hard capped at 40, is there any planed limit to the lifetime total of the amount of gold earned on saver seas? I absolutely think there should be, I don't believe a simple nerf to the amount of gold earned is enough to dissuade a large amount of the playerbase to simply farm 100% of their gold on saver seas without any threat of pvp. There's not really that much to even buy in the game with gold, by the time a player has gotten to level 40 in reputations they would have gotten enough gold for an entire ship/weapon/clothing/equipment set and will be further unmotivated to ever step foot into the high seas as the cosmetics you actually unlock from the maxing the trading companies are rather limited vs what you can just flat buy with gold. I think there should be a limit of 500k gold earned, ever. Enough to buy a captained galleon if they save their money, or a sloop and a few cosmetics. '

-Are special event gold multiplier bonuses applied to saver seas? Including Gold & Glory, Community Day, Gold Rush or any other special event that multiplies the gold value of items sold Again I think all of these should also be limited to high seas only. Especially Community Day, where's the community when you're off just playing alone on your own server? I always thought the idea behind any gold multiplier event was to get a lot of players playing at the same time, if all they are doing is motivating players to login to their own private servers then what's the point? If all these bonuses are on saver seas again players will have even less motivation to play on the high seas during these events, even with the saver seas gold penalty. -Are special event tasks able to completed on saver seas? One-off events such as the current "Wish you were here" event To me I think this should largely depend on the event itself. Some events are designed more around player interaction or loot gathering and I think should be limited to high seas while others that are mostly just sailing/exploring would be fine on saver seas. Some events could be mixed to where you can get most of it done on saver seas but have to go to high seas for the last few tasks to get 100% of the event cosmetics. I've always liked events that have you interact with other crews but choose to how you do it, such as "Give 5 Captain's Chests to another crew and have them turn them in OR Steal 5 captains chests from another crew". I'm fine with some events being available on saver seas, what I don't want to see is events like I mentioned never being done ever again on the high seas because now 100% of the events are being designed to also be able to be completed on the saver seas. -Is the kraken available on saver seas? Just curious, assuming yes. '

Are ALL commendations (other than those specific to high seas only features) able to be unlocked on saver seas? From the presentation the answer to this was yes. This is absolutely my biggest problem with the current saver seas, I think this is giving way, way too much of the total available "content" to saver seas. I look at the game's new progression: Level 40 reputation (saver seas), PL and max reputations (high seas), "End game" commendation grinding (saver seas). The only end game SOT has is either PvP or commendation grinding, in terms of gameplay hours getting max reputations is a small fraction of the time it takes to grind commendations ( this coming from someone who has done virtually every commendation in the game, this is 100s of hours of gameplay ). Allowing ~80% of the commendations to be completed on saver seas is going to permanently remove a portion of the veteran playerbase, not just new players and casuals, from the high seas servers. I don't think progress for the base 3 factions (Gold Hoarders, Order of Souls, Merchant Alliance) should be available on saver seas, I think all progress for these should be locked to the high seas only. I think the only available commendations available to be unlocked on saver seas should be Tale Tales, Hunter's Call, and Bilge Rats. '

Overall I think there is far too much of the game's overall progress being made available to be completed on saver seas. I am 100% for giving players a place to complete Tall Tales, learn base game mechanics, teach their friends the game, or play with young family members in a safe space free from the threat of pvp. I think however that there's way too much beyond that being provided, which is going to permanently remove a larger portion of the playerbase from the high seas servers. I think there should be a hard cap on gold earned, world events should be on high seas only and the commendation progress should be severely limited on saver seas. If the goal is to give newer and casual players a safe place to learn the game then I think there is way too much progress available on saver seas that is going to also drag away a large amount of veteran player's playtime. ' (edited)

I play the game as a Hunter. I don't pvp for the sake of doing pvp, my motivation and enjoyment of the game comes from stealing other people's treasure, from playing like an actual pirate . This requires that I'm able to find other players that are seeking treasure, not players that are seeking pvping for the sake of pvp or other Hunters. On paper saver seas could potentially be good for Hunters, likely removing the majority of tall tale-ers and players that don't understand the base game mechanics. I'm sure this is the part of the intention. There is also a very real possibility that this change could effectively ruin the game for players that want to play as Hunters. I sincerely hope this isn't the case. However I have very strong fears that far too much content is being made available to the saver seas, potentially transforming the high seas into a wasteland for Hunters. If the majority of other ships are just doing hourglass and run from every fight or just out hunting themselves, if it starts taking 2-3 hours to ever find another player ship with any treasure to steal, then the game's effectively dead for me. This has always been the main worry and argument against ever adding any form of PvE servers to the game.

Hello! We're gearing up to prepare for our 2023 Awards event to celebrate the game and its community. We're particularly drawn to the idea of filming this event on Mêlée Island due to its breathtaking scenery. However, we're facing a logistical challenge: we can only send one crew to the island, which means we'll be limited to showcasing just three individuals in each scene. While we have a plethora of creative ideas for filming across various areas of the island, this limitation doesn't quite capture the essence of "community" we'd like to showcase. Is there any possibility of making Mêlée Island available on custom servers and enable more than one crew on the island? I understand that this might be a very long shot, but it never hurts to inquire, right? Have to say that it's unfortunate that these fantastic locations from APL and MI cannot be accessed within the shared world, allowing interaction with other crews beyond the confines of the tall tales

It would still be instanced and so not available on custom servers. We can't just add islands to the map and I honestly don't think we can just give it as an option for load in. It definitely wouldn't be doable quickly or without a dedication of resources.

So I already see this going around and it's worth clarifying: When drew says "it might be slower to attempt quickswapping now than switching normally", is this intended? As in, is Rare actively punishing people for using their existing muscle memory?
I think basing anything on what's currently on insiders isn't a good idea as it's always subject to change. I'm not a dev but just offering my anime backstory.

We aren't actively punishing anyone no, but i can see how you might get to that given i don't give that clarification. At this stage it's really a by-product of the wielding/animation systems and i feel we've likely taken this system as far as it's going to go - I'm unconvinced that optimising further is actually possible. What i think players ultimately need to take from this is that unlearning the muscle memory of Quick Switching, while it's going to be a challenge is best path to the smoothest gunplay experience. We'll keep our eyes on this topic as expected once we launch to make sure this beds in as we hope it will! (edited)
appreciate the clarification. I figured it was likely a by-product but I already know we're going to get asked about it 100 times
I'll be sure to relay it wasn't intentional
Yeah it's in no way some form of punishment/hinderance to those with the muscle memory, i think if we could have seen a route to fixing it we would have done it already.

Thank you @Christina for the message in #announcements I think this level of immediate communication to the Sea Of Thieves Community is exactly what is needed at this time. One area that I think may also deserve a few words is around the recent rise in server crashing by nefarious agents who are only looking to ruin the experience of the player base. There are screenshots and videos circulating and it only serves to fuel the narrative that SoT is broken. I understand the difficulty in wanting to make sure that a spotlight isn't given to the idiots doing this but, at least from what feelings I am picking up from my community, the players aren't feeling safe sailing the seas and Season 10 has not changed that. Appreciate all the work the Rare team (and all the contracted teams!) are doing for the game. We all want to see big wins here I am sure. ❤️

In response to @j0toro post in the #rare-answers channel concerning Insiders... I'll state this again about making Insiders non-NDA. I truly feel you would get a lot more testing if people were allowed to talk about the current build(s) on Insiders outside of the forums. Things like Monkey Island & PotC of that nature can remain NDA and have it's own special testing if need be, but if you're TRULY wanting to get an accurate feedback on bugs/exploits on Insiders before it's released to retail, removing the NDA is the way to go. Picture this... Jake loads into Insiders prior to Season 8 and sees the new Hourglass Mode ready to be tested. Since it's no longer NDA, Jake tweets out about it and lets his friends know, who then lets others know and words gets back to a streamer who then announces it and lets even more people know. Loads of people are loading into Insiders to test it out vs having to keep things hush-hush and on the downlow. But when it's kept NDA, you're gonna have a lot of people avoiding it completely just out of pure caution to not be associated with something that could get them banned because of a slip up. And this isn't just my take. I hear this all the time in my stream in regards to Insiders. Also, if you want to go a step further, and I honestly feel this is more important than the whole NDA thing, and that is remove the 1hr login reward and change it to actual testing. Make it a requirement to actually complete a voyage or world event tied into the current build on Insiders that's needing to be tested. You could even go the extra mile and reward people with even further gold, doubloons or cosmetics for finding game breaking bugs and reporting them. But currently?... A lot of people log in and idle for an hour to get that reward and log out, without testing a thing. You'll never get accurate testing as long as that continues to be a thing. I hope these suggestions are really considered. I want this game to improve. I think we all do.
Aw man, I love the idea of bug-bashing in Insiders with comsetics as the reward. Truly helps the game dev and rewards those who are fully invested!

I really don't even understand the point of the NDA on Insiders when anyone that owns the game can join. Anything important always has and always will be leaked. It just doesn't make sense to me when I've never seen any other game do this. Most other games will often have a "Public Test Build" that players are allowed to discuss openly as well as even stream. I don't see the purpose of the NDA when the vast majority of changes are mechanical ones. If it's simply to avoid spoilers of the (very limited) story components of the game then players can just take it upon themselves to avoid spoilers. A lot of the testing that is needed for features is PvP/Multiplayer testing and it's currently very difficult for anyone to ever organize time with anyone else to test anything as your only ever allowed to even discuss Insiders in any way with anyone else except on the forums. People have been banned for just writing DMs to other insiders . So you can't ever talk about it with anyone, even other Insiders, except on the the Forums and direct voice communication (which they could record and still get you banned). This all makes it extremely inconvenient to test any multiplayer features on Insiders ever. Like what are the perceived benefits of even having Insiders under NDA? For myself I'm done with Insiders, I have my 4 year trinket. I only test the things I personally care about now, most of which are PvP mechanics that I can never organize another person to actually help me test anyway. Software Testing is what I did for a living, you want more testing out of me then I need a paycheck

DbD is the best example, players are allowed to stream the Public Test Build and yes it generates a lot of discussion and hype for the next update. Insider NDA just doesn't make any sense to me from any kind of business standpoint, what is the actual point of it?

What are the chances that blunderbomb naval turning would be changed as you are going hard on exploits and similar technical issues? But due to bug/feature it can either be pulled in or pushed away depending on both of your coordinates compared to the center of the map

I'm going to give you the full answer on this cos I think it's important. Christina has pushed for us to do an inclusive pride flag or a trans flag for a long time - we have seen so much noise on it, so many threads, so many players who would love it. And we would love it. But some territories would not be able to sell the game if there were more overt references. Using the Community Day flag in these colours was our way of showing solidarity with our trans players in places where the official flag is banned. Please don't quote this answer but feel free to rephrase it and explain should you be asked but it's a very PR loaded question and answer so we haven't said anything official other than our blatant support across channels and company ideals.

I'd love to add a bit more context to this, just because I want it to be super clear: if we could put a trans flag in, we would, and it pains me that we can't. True story, we actually made an inclusive Pride flag - but the Xbox team that checks content for Global Readiness would not let us ship it (if we were to in certain territories, there would be relatively severe, potentially legal consequences). The current rainbow flag is only in there because it's 'just' a rainbow flag with no nod to the cause behind it in the description - and therefore something we have been able to 'get away' with. After a lot of exploration on how we could do this best, I'm really glad that we ended up getting our support in for our trans players communities via this way - and we've tried to push that in the messaging as hard as possible without actually saying 'trans' (because rules). The fact that Community Weekend is something that is so visible for Sea of Thieves players, and at its heart is about community and belonging, makes it such a meaningful vehicle for this show of support. I'll be honest, it is rubbish we can't do it properly - in a perfect world, we would, but this world is far from perfect, so we have to do our best within the parameters we have been given. We're doing things with the franchise to broaden our inclusivity efforts - especially where game content rules don't restrict us (like the comics!), but we know we can always do more, and it's a conscious part of how we operate to look at ways we can be better. Tl;dr - we are trying our best and will continue to do so, and I'm so sorry that content restrictions don't allow us to say the words out loud. The Community Weekend flag with the right colours was the best, most meaningful solution to a global readiness conundrum. As Jo's said, this is a contentious topic - and everything I've shared here I would appreciate being kept private/under NDA - but felt like you deserved that additional context.

Thanks for explaining this (I was curious myself), that really sucks that you're in this position and I'm sorry you're held back by contracts... contracts & rules that exist and state that you cannot legally say the word "trans" or "gay" is everything that is wrong with this world (edited)

Hihi! I'd like to make a suggestion to Rare regarding future sponsored streams by higher profile content creators. Let me start by saying I totally understand why marketing would want to put a highlight on SoT with the recent PS5 launch and that dollars (or pounds ) talk when it comes to visibility. I'd would love to see a closer integration with the established partners/affiliate communities. I took a look at three sponsored streams today (Cohh Carnage, Felicia Day, Jesse Cox) and it's pretty clear that none of them regularly play the game. Again, totally fair! But if the opportunity here is visibility, and if their communities have questions that these creators can't answer, or maybe they miss an opportunity to showcase something cool because they just don't know about it, I think there's room for one of us at any given time to maybe act as a deckhand, providing insightful context. This is also where I would love if a greater focus was on the custom servers. A few of these sponsored streams stuck to safer seas, to avoid player interaction. But if we could get them all together on a custom server and have some of us on hand, directing traffic as it were, we would have a massive collaboration and I think a better opportunity for virality. Just imagine: two sponsored streamers, two members of this discord on six ships with an organized event like Cutlass Crusades or Race of Legends. It would really show off a lot of what our community has to offer! (edited)

Horn of Winds Could the commendation to "sell X number of horns" be adjusted. With today's nerf to farming them the commendation is extremely out of touch of the rarity of the item. Player sentiment is universal that its too high and it forces an unhealthy play to anyone looking to complete that commendation. Once apon a time when an update arrived with new commendations they could realistically be done within the life cycle of that update. But since Captaincy there's been this attitude of "numbers go brrrr" and when people complain its met with "its not intended to be done in 1 day." I have friends who used to do commendations with organic gameplay. But now days have lost all care because the numbers are over the top. And such high numbers is heavily Incentivising the blight of cheese / exploit tutorials because they're just not reasonable. Player looks at commendation. Realizes its going to take a life time. Searches YouTube "how to do X fast" gets exploit cheese guide. Does it. Please consider adjusting the amount of Horns required to be sold to a reasonable number. And looking forward to future commendations to please not be so heavy handed with them. (edited)
^ Agree. Considering that a lot of the game mechanics have been adjusted for the people with less time to play, the 250 Horns sold seems not balanced to be realistic for those people.
Feedback heard loud and clear, will give the discussion a nudge.
Cheers dude, appreciate the consideration ❤️ (edited)

There's no question that the captaincy trinkets have greatly contributed to the decreased game client performance. These are high level of detail models that all have to be loaded into memory from the HD when a rival ship comes into range, usually causing a very noticeable stutter/freeze on the game client. Removing the trinkets should also cause a slight general increase in FPS when in CQC on an enemy ship. In a navel fight you will never see the rival trinkets anyway and when boarding an enemy ship in a PvP scenario the movement is so fast paced that you will usually never have time to even see the trinkets on a rival ship. I would assume most PvP focused players such as myself simply do not care what trinkets rival ships have on them and would much prefer to have even slightly improved game client performance by disabling them. (edited)
(I know it's Friday, my ideas don't necessarily come on the beginning of the work week. If you don't comment till next week it's fine. I did at least check the time of day before pinging )

All good! I didn't reply right away cos I went to speak to Engine about it. It's not something that's really feasible given the size of the team and the workload involved to make this an option. In regards to the recent post that was just made, what improvements can be made to the Support System on the website? Currently... It's a hot mess. Half the time the URL errors The other half it doesn't remember your login when every other website you use on the same browser does and when you log in, it redirects you to another page on the website away from the actual support form We, the players, are expected to provide proof and that should never be the case. We should only have to give a name and the Support Team should be able to then look at the GT's session and be able to detect if there's any erratic movement or anything outside the norm from the rest of the playerbase. Something should stick out like a sore thumb to indicate that a player is a cheater within the "code" The file submission is only 50mb. There are thumbnails bigger than that size. Very difficult to provide evidence given those requirements All in all, I just feel like the current support system that's available to us turns more people away from reporting than it does encouraging people to report. (edited)

It is clear that there are no definitive solutions to the problems of cheaters. But right now your ad suggests something really good. Unfortunately there will always be workarounds on their part but it's a good way to go
On the other hand, I find that there is a lack of concrete announcements concerning in-game harassment which clearly on "X" is something that has really been highlighted in recent weeks.

We, the players, are expected to provide proof and that should never be the case. That is one major and fairly common issue indeed. When new-ish players ask about reporting and are met with "you need video proof or it will be ignored", they are often left in a sad state as they had no proof. And they don't understand why they need to record it in the first place
Exactly, and I feel like it just totally negates the whole point.

The other part of it is that some cases are either hard to record or aren't instantly visible in the fragment you have (not to mention mandatory name being visible). The lesson we need to take here is that we should have more deeply explored this as folks ended up doing in Live and discovered the full extent of the issue and we could have halted the release.
Surely people would have been futzing around trying to break the fix that was going to be applied for fishing rod launch.

Very much appreciate the transparency and how candid you are being with all this. It does feel like there was a lot of pressure to get this build out on time, as it was aligning with Community Weekend and Emporium sales.

Going from Drew's announcement to here: I guess my biggest question is how do we feel about the value and impact of Insiders? Are there enough players actually putting in reports right now to catch these things before it goes live? I'm sure everyone's heard the rhetoric: "Most Insiders log 1 Hr for their rewards and hop off." I'd love to know if this is actually true. Because if it is, I think we need a differently incentivized program.

If the question is 'Could we get more value from Insiders' i think we 100% could get more value from the programme in key areas. Don't get me wrong, the programme continues to be incredibly valuable already in a lot of areas, but pre-release hardening and quality control isn't one of them.
Okay, that's great to hear!

Perhaps more guided advice to Insiders as to what they could or should be specifically checking may give better results. If everyone was told they'd only get their reward if they did an hour of hitting random voyages or messing with fishing rods, it may get them to at least play and you'd get the telemetry data on what is happening

I totally get the push and also wish we had the capability behind the scenes to be able to reward based on completing specific actions, but the overhead effort to achieve this and the flexibility it would need to manage as a programme has always been too high I'm afraid. Part of the challenge of releasing Insiders so frequently is that our processes need to be lean and flexible (we hit Insiders build 710 this week!)

How come this issue with Hanging on Ledges wasn't caught earlier?
I will always have the teams back for making the tough call to not release a feature until it is ready
I would rather a feature be complete and ready before it gets into the game

I think in this instance there's been some masking going on, where there is a known issue with a predictable solution but it's ultimately hiding further work behind it. Once the known issue is resolved both the change itself always adds the potential for risk but in this case opens up a range of testing scenarios that has just generated further work. For such a small feature it has generated some significant impact player facing issues recently. At the point we've made the call we know the known work is going to push us past our launch date, but more importantly there are good signals to indicate there is further work still to be found. The Team are rallying on the fixes but more importantly stepping back through the feature from a deeper test perspective to ensure we rebuild that confidence back up again before we bring it live.

Delaying a feature that has known issues vs pushing it out solely for the sake of it being "on schedule" is ALWAYS the correct decision. Myself and I believe the vast majority of the playerbase will respect this far more than having things delivered on time that included major PvP exploits. So to you on this @Sonicbob (Drew)

Hello, well given the announcement made today, I really wonder why you don't want to take 2 or 3 months off from the release of the seasons to allow you to work properly on the health of the game and properly check the future content. I really like and respect your work and you probably made a good decision today, but I'm sorry, I won't be able to defend the studio this time if people complain about 75 being disabled % of S14 content. It would certainly have been necessary to postpone it as was already the case for a previous season. I understand that as a company, you need to release new content on the game in order to keep your players active... But maybe this is not a good approach because you still lose players who end up being disappointed with the decisions made by the studio which greatly impact the quality of life of the game. I'm just a simple player and it's difficult to put myself in the shoes of a studio obviously, but as a player, I tell you, there are certain things that need to be improved and in all sincerity, I I find it hard to believe that improving internal testing will change much if you stick with a new season formula every 4 months with so many new features for Sea of ​​Thieves.

I wasn’t expecting such difficult news from the video and today’s update. I can only imagine how challenging it must be for your team to navigate everything, and the last thing you need is more complaints from us. However, it’s hard not to feel frustrated, and I apologize for expressing that. Season 13 was already quite rough at launch, with numerous glitches that impacted gameplay. The atmosphere felt heavy with negativity, and after a while, I had to take a three-week break from Sea of Thieves because it became difficult to enjoy or create content around the game. I was really looking forward to Season 14, hoping that the fun and lighthearted vibe would return. I felt motivated again, but sadly, the start of this season has been even more challenging. I’m not sure if making the Insider program public, as some suggest, is the right solution—or if there's another approach. But something clearly needs to change to improve the release process and minimize frustrations for everyone involved. I appreciate the difficult decision that was made today. It must not have been easy, especially at the beginning of a new season, but I understand that the severity of the bugs left no choice. While the update video gave us some short-term insights, I’d love to learn more about your long-term plans. Is this going to be the norm moving forward? Is Sea of Thieves in such a state that this pattern might continue? What measures do you plan to take to ensure smoother releases in the future? Can we hope for a fully stable build one day? And where do the developers see the game’s performance a year from now? As content creators, we’re not only interested in new features and updates, but also in the overall health and longevity of the game. This affects our professional status and future plans, especially for those of us who make a living from creating content around the game. Once again, I truly appreciate the tough call made today. It’s undoubtedly the right decision, but I have to admit, it does leave me feeling concerned about the future. Love to all ❤️
All i'll say is, the plant in the background of the video needs watering.

I do want to say, as a point of positivity, that underneath the jank of bugs and exploits returning the core experience of the new features are a lot of fun. They are goofy and delightful. And I don't think people would have minded if there was a slight delay on this season being launched if it saw it coming out in a much more stable state.

Yes, it must be clarified, the new content that we have been able to test is so fun, and that is what will inevitably create frustration in having deactivated them, because people love these new things.

Testing objectives instead of 1 hour for the reward should be made. Or implement a title for those who report x amount of bugs. Hell, imagine having a brave Vanguard recolour as a reward for doing this. 1 bug a week and has to be over 50 weeks to earn them. Poor CS mean but at least it would encourage bugs to reported. I also find the submitting a ticket flow poor. It's a typical CS page that has you often searching before you can raise a request. It's a barrier of entry that needs removing.

While sounding great on paper, I'd say that reporting bugs for additional rewards is a terrible idea. Call me a negative one, but I've seen a fair amount of communities and cliques of people that are dedicated in obtaining such rare titles/rewards with less than noble intentions and means. It could (and would) likely lead to mass reporting of a bug without trying to reproduce it, leading to mass reports of low hanging fruits bugs. Looking back at my bug reporting from Insider testing, if I remember correctly, none were adressed and some went straight to retail, even after providing reproducing step etc. I'd vouch for approach similar to Deep Rock Galactic with pleasefix site, where players can submit bugs and another player can try to retest those to confirm reproduction possibility. This would help out with assessing how many players can encouter said bug, scale of the problem and possibly, comment on those. Additionally, players would know what more needs to be checked upon after inital testing with Your guidelines. This of course, may have drawbacks(knowledge of breaking stuff), but I believe pros could outweigh this con. Sorry for lengthy comment!

That wouldn‘t change anything. People leaking don‘t care about the NDA. People withholding information will withhold if there is a NDA or not. That said, I‘m neither on the side of people asking to remove the NDA nor people thinking it should remain. But a few weeks ago I‘ve suggested the following: „The main issue are two things: Rewards for idling one hour at an Outpost. And not having rewards for actual testing. So I was thinking... When looking left and right... There are commendations for the Creator Programme. Only visible for those who achieved a grade of one of the commendations. Maybe adding insider commendations to the live build will motivate more people. I.e. a commendation for either a helpful bug report or other feedback you / Rare seem worthy to get a +1 to the counter. Also the game has so many counters, and collecting so much data about activities. Define the test activity for a specific build and people successfully finishing the activity will get another +1 to their counter. I could imagine it would be possible to add "endless" commendations, only unlocking after reaching grade V of the previous one. Every Grade V commendation could come with rewards for the live build. Including titles, maybe a few Ancient coins and cosmetics.“

Building out commendations is something we have discussed multiple times internally but it's not feasible. Those commendations would have to be set up for new things every build, that's 8 times amonth on top of commendations for retail. While we love the idea of tying rewards to actions, that's far, far toomuch work for us to sustain (edited)

Also not to mention if it only pops if your report is helpful - who is checking that to then tie it to a game session and gamertag or do we need tobuild a whole new process and program to do it? You see where itisn't as easy as we would like

Simply allowing Insiders to be streamed would absolutely increase the amount of issues found before launch to live. The idea that some people would still find and hide the knowledge doesn't mean that some additional issues would be brought public with enough time for them to be actioned. Removing the NDA on Insiders won't fix every single issue, but it would IMPROVE the amount of issues found. People talk like every single thing has to be all or nothing, it's a world of grey folks, it's not black and white. SOT isn't a story game. The one major story update the game did get (The Pirates of the Caribbean) was hidden from everyone anyway, including Insiders. Games like WOW, DBD and many other games have a Public Test Server that can be openly streamed. Countless issues are found by the communities of these games well ahead of launch. Being able to stream the test server also generates hype ahead of time for upcoming updates. All critical Insiders information is just leaked on Twitter anyway, no NDA is ever going to stop this. I've said from the beginning that the NDA on Insiders is simply a bad business decision that does more harm than good and I really hope that this seasons disaster of a launch is enough to finally convince the correct people of this. (edited)

@j0toro ^ Haven't poked you about anything in a while so I'm due. TL:DR Briggsy Quest lets you respawn with no minimum distance from other ships even if the other ships aren't on the quest, should only give min distance respawns when ALL ships involved are on the quest at the same time.

Hello, This is a technique we are aware of. While it was not a direct function we wanted when making this feature. It currently is not something that we are at this stage actively acting against. We are continuing to monitor the situation and things may change as we continue to work on the Live service, but as of current we are neutral towards it. Hope that helps.

Well I would consider it a Major PvP Exploit . There are absolutely players that are aware of this and abuse it. But that's just my opinion I guess. Unless I'm missing something it could also be fixed very easily with a single line of code, just an IF statement to check if another boat within minimum distance is also on the quest. In general I really wish the prevailing attitude was more along the lines of "This is a PvP exploit and should be fixed" vs "We don't really see that many people using this and don't think it's that bad". I'd really like to see PvP issues get more attention instead of waiting for exploits to be used by over 50% of the playerbase before they're seen as being important enough to address. (This is about as UN-snarky as I get, I am merely voicing my opinion)

So I've hit this in a few places, but consider this team currently at Skeleton crew size right now and reaching full capacity in the next month or so. It's taken longer than we'd anticipated but that's how resourcing and onboarding happens sometimes. The Skeleton crew have been mostly diverted to exploit fixes and cheat detection though recently due to us prioritising these areas, they are now beginning to pivot to Game Performance as the next big area we are focusing on.

Please forgive me if I'm mistaken, but wasn't it said during season 12s video that this team was fully up and running? Perhaps I am mis remembering but I thought it was said that rare is now a big team and for the first time there is a team fully committed to game health. I've been telling people for 6 months that there's a team working on game health and I've had multiple disagreements with many different people who claim there was no game health team and that rare were just saying what we wanted to hear. I've been very vocal that they're wrong and the team were already hard at work and we would see results soon etc I get that these things take time and I respect that, but surely it wasn't the right call to tell the community that there was a dedicated team when this was not in fact the case and we are arguably in the most turbulent time the game has ever been through. I personally don't like being wrong about something and not being able to hold my hands up and say "I was wrong" But thinking back on how many arguments I've had and how much I've defended rare and the hard work the new quality of life team are doing, to find out they don't actually technically exist yet puts a really sour taste in my mouth (edited)

Hey - I think there's just a bit of nuance here that's got missed along the way. In August's news I shared an update on the Game Health team but was clear that this team was now 'starting to form' rather than any indicators that they were at full capacity. This team has been in operation since then but as a Skeleton team and have been responsible for a number of high profile fixes like Crud Launching, Cannon TP Detection and other behind the scenes detections. We'd hoped they'd have been at full capacity by now, however resourcing and onboarding isn't always that predictable. Appreciate there's some nuance to my update that maybe hasn't landed clearly, but we have already seen value from this team even at their smaller scale - I don't feel you've misrepresented the situation here.
