Is there a way that Partners can be brought in with assisting in the decision making process? I'm seeing a lot of "I wish we would have done it this way" for Partner's personal reasons or for the sake of the community. I feel like we could offer a lot of insight on what the community would appreciate if we were given the opportunity to consult with you guys before things are put out. "We're planning on doing X event, which of these rewards/cosmetics would get people more excited" "We're planning on doing X event, how can we make it successful for the community" "We're planning on adding in more story elements in a few seasons, which ones do you guys think we could benefit from building on" I'm not saying Oh yOu gUyS ShOuLd lEt uS MaKe yOuR ChOiCeS. We've been around for a while and love the game, and we have communities that love it just as much, and I genuinely feel like we have a wide base of opinions that could help if given the opportunity to do so in a proactive way rather than a reactive one. (edited)
We're all covered under NDA, so there's no additional risk to bringing us into the fold proactively, right?

We're Partners because we love the game and want to do our best to contribute, or at least I'd hope that's the case. I genuinely think that the benefit of bringing us in to help guide would increase the excitement in the community, and give all of us a sense of pride in each season/event we were able to contribute to.

Rare lives and breathes the game on the backend. We live and breathe it on the front. I think we could all benefit from working together as a cohesive unit. We can be an asset to development and growth if you guys are willing to use us.

I've admittedly already asked this question before, but hear me out. My entire purpose in this game is to bring people together and remind them why they started playing in the first place. In an effort to do so, I have introduced players I have met on the seas to the memorial of James White 83 times (by my count.) I understand that it isn't a metric that is tracked publicly, and I'm not saying this to brag. I do this because at the end of every Glitterbeard, I know that I was able to take a group of people's shitty day and make it better. Is there any way to actually find out how many times I have been able to do that? Is that recorded somewhere? I am getting closer to Glitterbeard 100, and that means I have introduced over 400 players to the memory of the man who (from what I understand) shared my vision for what this game should be, and that means a lot to me. I would love for that number to be accurate so I can reflect on the good I have been able to do. Is there anyone who can help me to determine that number? I don't do all of this and put all of this effort in for clout or for holding records. I do it because I want to be an example of the type of thing that CAN and SHOULD exist on the seas, and I would love to know how effectively I am pursuing that goal.
This would actually be a really nice metric to add to community weekends, or just "This year in SoT". Have a total amount of times people have activated Glitterbeard.
(also, please Fix Glitterbeard, we did it this evening, and the tree is broken)

There's a theory going around that fresh accounts experience less lag cause it won't have to load all cosmetics. How much truth is there to this?

I would like to hear Rare’s answer on this as well, but coming from someone that has about 30 alt accounts from all the challenges and speed runs I have done, there are 2 things you’re guaranteed on new account: Faster Purchase Time More Fresher Crews on Initial Server Faster Purchase Time meaning that when you go to purchase supplies from the Merchant vendor, the “counting coins” is almost instant and there is a very small wait time to actually have the crate appear in your hands after retrieving it from the vendor as well. As far as lag goes, that’s an urban legend. Server stability is terrible all around. Whether it’s on a fresh account in Adventure Mode, on a Custom Server, doing a Tall Tale in a dedicated instance or even in Safer Seas by yourself… the lag is there regardless across the board and its server stability. Something has changed over the years and it continues to get worse from one update to the next. But again, I would LOVE to hear what Rare’s answer is to your question. (edited)
Can +1 this, experienced the same too.
100% about buying supplies/anything being faster. I reported this in this discord years ago
Will also note, that in high server load, if you have any cosmetics on your weapons at all, it actually stablizies FPS more than having a skin on your gun/sword.

Could I please get some clarification on the following: Therefore, if an external solution providing Crosshair tools does become flagged by one of these security systems, players may find themselves under enforcement for its use. Does this mean that at a certain point SOT may decide to add "crosshair V2" (steam application) to their detections, or is this more meant in a way that if certain software actually tampers with the game files it may accidently get flagged as cheating software?

No not in this scenario - what I'm trying to protect folks from here is that EAC for example is operated by Epic and runs a suite of detections for known malicious software across its whole game portfolio. If you begin using a Crosshair tool that for some reason comes under enforcement by EAC for example (likely due to that software also doing something naughty behind the scenes) then this will indirectly cause you to receive enforcement across the EAC portfolio. I think many isolated Crosshair tools wont fall foul of this (and clearly haven't so far), but it's a warning that you do this at your own risk as this will be an enforcement outside of direct Sea of Thieves control.

Hey guys, I just want to gently point out that while I can understand the humour some may find in this, it can also be incredibly dangerous and we would appreciate these not being shared online or publicly. This kind of stuff can give bad actors terrible ideas that could lead to the abuse of our team as they manipulate them into saying things via AI. I know this is meant in jest and 'this is clever' but concerns have been brought to us, so please be aware of these valid concerns that have been raised.
As someone who was a victim of a fake VC call, voice cloning people without permission can lead to legal action being taken. Which is what I did, don't do it.

I hope it's understood I respect all the folks at Rare and never meant to cause any distress. I've removed the content. I do believe though that this sort of technology, used appropriately, is beneficial in the long run. As a long time fan of Star Trek, I've always wanted a universal translator in my head. So seeing this starting to come to life was just a Wow moment for me I wanted to share. But admittedly I didn't think of ulterior motives. Alas where we do have to worry about that, huh?

Excluding the fact that such a tool is dangerous, as Jo said, the potential for faster translations... yeah is good but that AI remains an automated system and necessitates the same rigorous translation review process by human, ultimately offering little practical advantage for us. It doesn't save time...
Just get yourself a babel fish

Oh I ABSOLUTELY get it pal, dont worry! And I agree in that AI has a place as a tool but not a replacement and that's where we should be careful, especially as creative people in a creative industry. I 100000% know this was not meant with malice and that you respect the team but many of us have been targeted in game and out of game and the best thing I can do in my role is act as first line of defence and have these conversations. We've had partners targeted with deep fakes and AI abuse as well so my job is to live in paranoid land but it is for good reason. Please understand this was not targeted, nor was it meant to make you feel bad and I apologise if it has. We respect you as much as you respect us (edited)

People grow and change Things can be said in the heat of the moment Second chances are valid Removing them on one comms interaction likely leads them down pipelines to ban evasion and cheating In game moderation is a careful tightrope to balance to protect our players, uphold standards and allow people chances to make mistakes and grow and learn. Me, like Sayuta, had access to the partners' channels. I can understand the frustration of the unknown, in this case, the topics discussed with the partners during the calls, but believe me, apart from what they say to all of us—partners and affiliates—the rest of the calls for them are about other matters, not the game. Yes, it's true, the communities members are more prone to questions about the game's status, but knowing about the channel linked by Sayuta would be pointless because it's under NDA, and we can't share anything until we get the green light. Moreover, even if we could say more in the communities, it wouldn't change anything. People, even with all the explanations, will keep complaining and asking more and more with their whys anyway. So I prefer to use the phrase 'The team Rare is aware of this and is working on it. If you prefer, open a ticket to the support and ask to Rare what do you want to know '. Having meetings via calls would be like waiting for video content updates—one every three months. We wouldn’t have roadmaps, announcements, news, previews, or timely communications as we do now. So, going back to Becky's question: besides everything she and Jotoro share here, what exactly do you want to discuss in these "calls"? We already have all the news and communications we need.

Would you like to get a sense of how we affiliates are regarded? Just look at Drew asking affiliates for feedback on new features coming to the game or Jo and Becky being available even beyond working hours and responding if you message them privately. I’d say we are highly valued. (edited)

I want to be clear that I'm not challenging or upset with anyone or anything, but just want to throw in my two cents regarding this conversation: We've always heard about this "Partner Call" where they would discuss game updates, current game status, and future production etc. AA could never join because we are not up to Partner-level NDA. (I'm genuinely curious about the difference between Partner-level and AA-level NDA. I mean you already have my real life information/address/contact number ... do I have to provide my credit card number as well? (I'm joking plz don't hurt me)) I don't know why, but in March 2023 we were invited to join this call along with the partners. We went through the upcoming season 9 contents, sandbox changes, and more details about the hot-topic fixes etc. I thought the call was very informative and nice, we even had a Q&A section at the end where everyone could ask questions and get answers directly from the Rare team (Drew/Anna/Shelly). Both TK and I joined this call, and we privately discussed how the fixes would help in our future events, what new events we could create with the new content, and we were hyped about the game development. I was super excited about season 9 and felt included and confident in leading my community after the call. To clarify, I wasn't sharing any of the information to our community, but at least I had an idea of where the game and the development was headed. ...then that was it. We never had the same call after, ever . (It is what it is though ) I think most AA leaders care about the custom servers (hosting big events without a customizable custom server requires alot of manpower and time, and it is a nightmare if something goes wrong). So when we see that there is a scheduled Partner call to talk about the custom server, yet we are not invited nor any further update, it is understandable why some AA leaders would feel left out or perhaps less valued compared to the partners. An example of what "this and that" is would be custom server & anti-cheats (before it actually came out). The game was literally unplable on Asia servers because of how often we would run into cheaters, and I recalled partners requested to talk about it in their partner call, where AA was left with "we cannot give more information right now". (Once again, I understand the reason behind it and not exactly complaining, just speaking from our point of view ) Buuuuuuuuuuuuuuuuuuuuuuuuuut with everything being said, I still take pride at being an Affiliated Alliance leader, I'm still more than happy to answer all the questions or troll comments from our community, and I still greatly appreciate all the hard work you've done for us & the attention we get from you guys. Everyone I've encountered at AA has been nice and helpful, always providing feedback and ideas of how each community operates. Also big props to Becky and jo for always replying promptly, Anna someone once took 7 months before replying my message. (I don't mind, I just thought it was funny (it wasn't anything important anyway)) Big thank you to @j0toro @beckyfr0st @H1gh Voltage I understand it must be alot of stress and unimaginable work load to deal with the community and us.

I've been part of the AA program for a few years now, and over time, some differences between us and partners have become noticeable, especially in terms of communication. We've seen that partners often receive certain information earlier than we do or have had access to materials sooner. These resources are also incredibly important to us, as we work on translations to ensure non-English speakers have easy access to trailers and key information. I remember taking part in a call two years ago, and it was great to finally be included in that kind of communication. In the past, there were even special events for AA representatives—not just on the official stream, but dedicated moments where we could engage, have fun, and connect with each other and the team. It really felt like our contributions were recognized. We put in a lot of effort to support our communities, and moments like those truly reinforced that we matter. That’s why I’d love to see the return of calls with some additional insights into your plans or the game's status—something beyond what insiders receive—especially since we're under a stricter NDA. When I first heard about a partner call happening after two years, I was excited! But then, realizing it was only for partners was a bit disappointing. If there are parts of those calls that are strictly about the partner program and not relevant to us, perhaps we could still be included in a general segment before that. It would be a great way to strengthen our connection and keep us engaged in the process. In the end, we all care about the same thing, we love this game, value our communities (even though it can be challenging :D), and want to keep doing our best

To clarify, many streamers in Sea of ​​Thieves use double input (using both MnK & controller input to rotate the steering wheel faster). Is it allowed for us to do this as well?

It doesn't actually make the steering faster at all. Visually it looks faster. HOWEVER. It DOES create a buffer for steering. How do I explain this..... So, when the faster animation ends, you can let go of the wheel, and the rudder will auto steer to the desired location that the animation perceived?
Basically, you can automate a hard turn, whilst you're below deck planking.

But it's a huge advantage when your steering wheel is triple-broken and you turn twice as fast. Even if the animation lags, you can let go of the wheel sooner and do other things. So it's an HUGE advantage.
Oh wow, thats a very valid point I did not think about.

I'm seeing alot of posts complaining about partners ''hiding'' the fact that they get ''Safe servers'' (Avoids putting them in servers with alt accounts) Is that even something that can be talked about publicly? Not sure if it's still a thing anymore
Yeah i've been asked that a few times over the last few days. Who's been peeping!
People seem to think it's public information because new parters openely talk about it? Just wanna make sure it actually is something public or NDA

I’m sure others will clarify but I’ve always had it in mind to not talk about it. But equally it’s not as bad as folks might be thinking. @Sonicbob (Drew) can correct me but as far as im aware simply put the game has Trusted Servers and Untrusted Servers. New players are unknown so typically get onto untrusted but, I believe, the more they play the more trusted they become and eventually get sorted onto trusted. So partners aren’t getting special treatment or anything. So why is it even a partner thing? It was just a combo of measures Rare took back around 2020 when Partners were being targeted daily by trolls. We made alt accounts with new names and Rare ensured we were flagged for trusted servers to have this layer of separation from the bad actors. It’s as simple as that. So there’s really no drama here. Just miss information I guess. (edited)

And I think that’s also why we didn’t really talk about it. It’s always a game of staying 1 step ahead of the bad actors. And while things aren’t anything like they were back then. Keeping the knowledge out of the public helped keep everyone safer.
So yea, if new partners are openly discussing it. Might be a point to re-clarify what is and isnt appropriate to speak about on stream.
And it’s worth reminding you would only be sorted into trusted servers at session creation. If you dive to anything or HG, you’re no longer on trusted servers, or there’s a chance you may not be.

So.....No - that's not quite right, but it's a good try It's a bit more complicated behind the scenes as servers/stamps aren't designated as 'Trusted'/'Non-Trusted'. Instead, our Matchmaking system can identify if trusted players are on a server and then make different decisions on which players can matchmake/migrate onto that server at that given time. This all ensures that we can maintain good server density as folks move in and out of servers during a servers lifetime. But echo @🚫Back on Wednesday🚫 's note earlier, this should be considered part of your NDA and not disclosed publicly.

The legend of the invisible lightning strike at the center of the map originates from Minecraft. In Minecraft, when an admin joined the game or banned a player, he would strike an invisible lightning that everyone could hear. This legend eventually made its way into Sea of Thieves as well. Over time, it became known as “the lightning strike means someone just joined the server.”

I do think it serves a purpose, especially since it strikes right at the center of the map, which the game seems to be technically anchored to. I wonder if it acts as a visible trigger for random events or other in-game mechanics. After all, the center of the map is essentially the game's beating heart.
I have the answer

In basic terms - Our lightning chooses someone to hit then strikes them 30secs later (i was not aware our lightning was The Predator like that) but if they're on the ferry, inside a tavern/shop/hideout or no longer on the server then it cant hit them so just hits point 0
Point 0 in a lot of games is where 'things gather'

I completely agree—something definitely feels off with the way things are going. To be clear, I’m not placing blame on the developers. The issue seems to lie more in the process itself. Feedback and bug testing become somewhat meaningless if a hardening build can pass QA, but then issues still arise when it's pushed to the live environment. What I’m really getting at is this: if the Insider program weren’t under NDA, there wouldn’t be a need for a separate build at all. You could simply launch the beta branch directly from the same executable. That way, any fixes made in the beta branch would carry over to the live version seamlessly. Plus, with the NDA lifted, discussions could happen publicly leading to broader engagement, more eyes on the problems, and ultimately, better feedback overall. The last part isn’t really up for debate it’s a well-tested and proven approach that benefits both the quality of feedback and the long-term sustainability of the game's hype. How is Microsoft itself doing absolutely nothing about this massive security vulnerability with the invite spam able to cause massive performance issues in the WINDOWS OS. It's been like this for years, people were complaining about invite spam F'ing their computer 5+ years ago... Xbox on PC continues to be one of the worst gaming platforms in existence. If it wasn't for SOT I would delete 100% of this Xbox S*** from my PC. I take it there's no real point in even contacting your MS partners over this issue?

Alert fatigue has always been a thing. Even with MFA push prompts threat actors would spam them just so people would accept them to stop them. You’ll have bad actors abuse legit services and to change that can seem like a simple change but the downstream effects can be major (think greater than just Xbox but Microsoft services in general). It goes deeper than that but generally that’s how larger F500 companies operate. It’s pretty much a lot of what I do professionally and there are a ton of moving pieces. Your points are true though so don’t take me as trying to discredit what you’re saying. Just wanting to give context that might help
Best solution: linux

Yes and no. In an enterprise environment you’re still going to have those problems but now you’re at the mercy of not just MS but whatever 3rd party you’re using to retrofit the management together. If you’re talking Desktop infrastructure we usually will use Macs since they’re unix at the core and more importantly you can manage it through JAMF or intune with having support. For every dev who can build support and manage a Debian/Ubuntu system for a dev environment there are 20 who don’t know how to use anything that isn’t .net. Not a bad thing since unless you’re a start up it’s cheaper for us to have an IT helpdesk etc troubleshoot a problem with a known environment vs the dev doing it themselves.
Sorry for clogging this up, but if anyone ever wants to talk this stuff for whatever reason send me a message.

The direction itself is irrelevant to meg spawns, there is no magic in it, it's simply that when you are sailing in Cardinal directions, you are more likely to cross over "pockets" on the map, increasing the chance of triggering a meg spawn, whenever the timer kicks in. This does not invalidate any of the previous discoveries, it actually enforces them, however, when I tell a player how to increase chances of meg spawns in a video, i cannot tell him "wait for one hour on a spot, until meg spawns", i encourage, when moving from one place to another , to stay in cardinal directions, to have a higher frequency of "pockets" below your ship due to the way they are placed around the map. For example, if you sail all the way from Q2 until J9, which is Ordinal, (South West) doesn't matter if its 100 hours, you will never get a meg.

I wanted to raise a few ongoing concerns about the Captaincy system that have persisted since the launch of Season 7. While I understand that not every issue can be addressed immediately and some things need to be deprioritized, it’s been over three years now with little visible progress on some core features, and many players are still experiencing the same frustrations. For example, it’s become increasingly frustrating to sail BLANK name of my ship and BLANK guild, only to repeatedly see that same problem on other ships. This happens across various situations: after diving, after using tunnels, after respawning from death, or even after black screens. It makes the core identity of a Captain and ship feel broken. Additionally, the logbook feature has felt mostly redundant. Logbooks have never worked quite right since launch, and now with diving being such a central gameplay mechanic, it’s rare to even see higher rank logbooks. When you do find one, it often contains only blank pages. Another issue that feels overlooked is the way Captaincy commendations and stats are updated. The Hunters Call and Reapers Bones have introduced multiple new loot and fish variations , yet none of these count toward existing Captaincy commendations. For example, catching a Stormfish during a Hunters Call voyage doesn’t update the “Stormfish caught” stat in the Captaincy menu. This inconsistency makes progression feel unrewarding for long-term and short-term players. I hear you that changes may be coming in future seasons, but it’s hard not to feel discouraged when a major feature like Captaincy, meant to be a meaningful long-term progression system, has been in a broken or incomplete state for so long. Is there any update on when we can expect meaningful improvements or fixes to these systems? More to the point, the countless other systems that have never worked (looking at you storage crates) I’m raising this not out of negativity, but out of a real passion for the game and a hope to see features like Captaincy reach their full potential.

I was hoping to ask, respectfully , if we could get some insight into the reasoning behind making events like Skull of Siren Song and the upcoming Heist in Month 2 "opt-in" world events. https://www.mouse-sensitivity.com/updates/updates/unreal-engine-4-editor-r766/ Something many have noticed, is that depending on your mouse polling rate, your sensitivity changes in sea of thieves. This is not normal for most games, and I think this thread explains why it’s happening, right now players have to lock their polling to 250Hz. Here’s a thread from sot where players noticed it a while ago. https://www.seaofthieves.com/community/forums/topic/158518/mouse-settings-improvements-and-fixes/2 I hope this gets fixed
man you speak in terms I've never heard of

As a ex competitive FPS player obsessed with settings (ex awper global elite on csgo with 125Hz polling rate, 400 DPI and 0.4 sens), I’m also well aware of this issue in SoT, but honestly, I don’t think it’s a problem that really needs fixing. We’re talking about a level of competitiveness that the game simply doesn’t have, because SoT isn’t an FPS — it’s a sandbox game for families or groups of friends. So it’s the kind of fix that only about 2% of players would even notice, basically the ones obsessed with TDM and doubleguns. When there will be a partnership with ESL or FaceIT, then it might be worth discussing. For now, we don't need this fix but, as me, you're free to use a mouse with 125Hz polling rate (edited)

Unpredictability is the mother of creativity. Sea of Thieves is loved for this, because with all its problems, it is an unpredictable game, nothing can be taken for granted, anything can happen. Every situation is 50% skills and 50% luck. If the game were perfect, we would see a gulf between skilled and poor players, which would make the outcome of every encounter a foregone conclusion.
Perfection would make Sea of Thieves a boring game.

anyway, I would love to see what the official position is on this, as the only thread about it has been ignored :( https://www.seaofthieves.com/community/forums/topic/158518/mouse-settings-improvements-and-fixes/2

its hard to explain this properly If I use my mouse normally: 1000hz polling >250, this means that sot applies mouse smoothing, resulting in negative mouse acceleration, meaning inconsistent sensitivity If I use my mouse on 250hz <=250hz, this means sot does not apply mouse smoothing, this means no negative accel, and consistent sensitivity great! except.. this means that I have more input delay as im using 250hz polling rather than 1000hz this does not cause stuttering but you have to choose between either input delay, or inconsistent sens by default, sot uses inconsistent sens this can be fixed by flipping a switch (as some users do already by changing an ini flag, which is a grey area...) allowing users to use 1000hz and no smoothing, meaning lower input delay and consistent sens
this isnt about high level pvp, this is about mouse gaming standards

Just circling back to the ongoing stability concerns. Between Seasons 13 and 18, there appears to be a noticeable reduction, possibly around 40%, in seasonal content output. But perhaps more pressing is the fact that overall stability seems to have declined during the same period. If the trade-off had been reduced content in exchange for improved performance and polish, I believe many in the community would have accepted that. Quality over quantity is a fair approach. However, what we’re currently experiencing feels like both less content and less stability. Monthly Adventures have stopped, seasonal content is being delivered more slowly, and what is being released often lacks the depth or excitement players are hoping for. On top of that, each patch increasingly seems to introduce new technical issues instead of resolving existing ones. At some point, we need to ask: When will things start moving in a more positive direction? This isn’t just long-time players talking, newer members of the community are starting to notice these trends too. The sentiment is growing, and it's getting harder to ignore. All we’re asking for is some open, transparent communication. Not marketing lines or vague reassurances, but a clear, honest update on what’s going on behind the scenes. What’s being done to address these issues? What should players reasonably expect in the short term? We want to support the team, but that’s only possible when we feel we’re being leveled with. Right now, it feels like that trust is starting to erode, not from negativity, but from silence. Also, whens the Season 18 call? Because we never got one for Season 17 (until after it was announced) Is our input on the update cadence no longer valid? Whats going on? Talk to us.

FoF - editing notes

That is a bit of a weird one as Bad GTs/Pets/Ships don't usually get "More Info." We either action them or we don't. These actually fall under the selection of tickets we can actually action via in game reporting. Someone may have accidentally sent the wrong response or the in-game reporting option was misclicked and not reported under Ship Name. We do also have a slight problem where players can be reported for bad ship names, when they are part of the crew. This means when investigating their account, they won't actually have any pets/ships at all, or there can be cases where players change the names of their pets/ships between the report being sent in. Regardless of these factors, the reports should still only be responded with a "Actioned" or "No Action" response.

I wanted to raise a genuine question around the recent Time Limited Events that have been introduced alongside Seasons. There’s a rumour circulating within the community that I’ve seen discussed across many platforms, livestream chats, YouTube comments, Twitter, and other community spaces. To be clear, this rumour isn’t based on any confirmed information. That said, some players are starting to feel concerned, myself included and others here, about the possibility that older time limited events could eventually replace new Acts in future Seasons, rather than Acts continuing to be brand-new content. So for example * "Season 21 - Act 1 Babboon Boss - Act 2 Smugglers Heist, again" * I’m bringing this up in good faith, not as criticism, but because with the upcoming dev direct, this feels like an opportunity to address the speculation early and hopefully ease concerns before they grow further. If the rumour isn’t accurate, a bit of clarity would go a long way. And if there is some truth to it, I think many players would appreciate understanding the reasoning behind that concerning direction. (edited)

As the devs have already said, the acts should be open to every playstyle, and that’s absolutely the right approach. If that’s the case, then the deeds tied to those acts also need to respect that idea. Any player should be able to complete them and progress toward cosmetics and renown regardless of how they choose to play. Because of that, the deeds can’t realistically be based on winning, losing, or even taking part in HG at all. The moment participation in HG becomes a requirement, it stops being open to every playstyle and starts pushing players into something they may not be comfortable with. That directly contradicts the idea that the acts are meant for everyone. So if the goal really is to keep things accessible to all playstyles, then the deeds have to focus on things that any player can do around HG without forcing them into that specific activity. Otherwise the system ends up favouring one playstyle while claiming to support them all. And theres ya problem! (edited)

Player A - Accumulates 50k doubloons and exchanges them for gold as intended by the developers before the reset was announced. As a result, they receive the sword as a reward commemorating the old doubloon system. Player B - Accumulates 50k doubloons but chooses not to participate in the doubloon economy for years. During the transition, they receive the commemorative sword for the old doubloon system and the gold equivalent of their doubloons. Why should Player B’s time be valued more than Player A’s? The current decision is the fairest for everyone and ultimately leads to the same outcome.

Honestly I don’t think that’s the real question here. The bigger issue is how completely erratic this whole system has become. Players just can’t keep up with what’s going on anymore, and that’s where the real problem is. If this had simply been the direction from the start, it probably wouldn’t have caused much of an issue. What’s frustrating people is the constant shifting of ideas and decisions. It’s not just this one thing either. It’s drops, updates, events, awards, date changes, delays. Everything feels like it’s always moving. Because of that, players are constantly confused and never really know what they’re supposed to trust. The trust that was already hanging by a thread took another hit with the announcement of the reset. At this point it just feels like beating a dead horse.
